name: Autophagy
description: Macroautophagy: starvation-driven, mTORC1-gated formation of a double-membrane autophagosome that engulfs cytoplasmic cargo and fuses with the lysosome for degradation and nutrient recycling.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Nutrient starvation"]
    N2["mTORC1 inhibited"]
    N3["ULK1 complex activated"]
    N4["PI3KC3-Beclin1: PI3P"]
    N5["ATG9 membrane delivery"]
    N6["LC3-I to LC3-II (PE)"]
    N7["p62 binds ubiq. cargo"]
    N8["Phagophore closes"]
    N9["Autophagosome formed"]
    N10{"Fuse with lysosome?"}
    N11["Autolysosome degrades cargo"]
    N12["Recycled amino acids/lipids"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 -->|yes| N11
    N10 -->|mature| N9
    N11 --> N12
    N12 -->|nutrients| N2

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff

nodeDetails.label: PI3KC3-Beclin1: PI3P
nodeDetails.detail: Class III PI3K/Beclin-1 generates PI3P to nucleate the phagophore

nodeDetails.label: ATG9 membrane delivery
nodeDetails.detail: ATG9 vesicles supply lipid to the growing phagophore

nodeDetails.label: p62 binds ubiq. cargo
nodeDetails.detail: p62/SQSTM1 bridges ubiquitinated cargo to LC3-II

nodeDetails.label: Phagophore closes
nodeDetails.detail: Membrane elongates and seals around cargo

nodeDetails.label: Autolysosome degrades cargo
nodeDetails.detail: Lysosomal hydrolases degrade the enclosed contents

nodeDetails.label: Recycled amino acids/lipids
nodeDetails.detail: Breakdown products returned to the cytosol

flowchartStandard.name: source_grounded_extraction_v2
flowchartStandard.sourceGrounding: Nodes, decisions, branches, and loops were extracted from the cited sources; each node carries a sourceRef to its supporting citation. Awaiting human spot-check before verified=true.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

sources.title: Autophagy: renovation of cells and tissues
Autophagy fights disease through cellular self-digestion
sources.authors: Mizushima, N.; Levine, B.; Cuervo, A. M.; Klionsky, D. J.
